reason: All 13 ADRs are present and Accepted. Template compliance (Status/Context/Decision/Rationale/Consequences) is complete for every entry. Citations with SAD.md and SRS.md line numbers are present in all 13 ADRs (HR-15 satisfied). Key decision alignments check out: ADR-P2-05 L3 LLM execution order matches SAD §3.9/§4.1 step-for-step (check_input → source_texts → sandwich → LLM call → grounding-on-output); ADR-P2-06 Option A keyword lexicon exactly mirrors SAD §3.7 (same keywords, same 0.2×count formula, same 1.0 cap); ADR-P2-10 priority-to-reason mapping matches SAD §3.11 (emotion_trigger=2, low_confidence=1, out_of_scope=0); ADR-P2-12 schema changes (emotion_history, edge_cases, ivfflat, three column activations, no DROP/ALTER TYPE) match SAD §5 and SRS FR-23 exactly. Alternatives are documented wherever required: ADR-P2-02 explicitly rejects hnsw with comparative rationale; ADR-P2-06 presents Options B and C in a structured table before selecting A. Rationale throughout is specific and testable, not vague. The SAD Round-2 LOW gap on FR-18 source path (§7 table vs §3.8) is a SAD-only concern; ADR-P2-08 correctly cites SAD §3.8 where both `dst/__init__.py` and `dst/tracker.py` are specified. One LOW gap is carried forward from the SAD §4.1/§4.2 escalation inconsistency: ADR-P2-10 lists two separate L4 triggers — 'HybridKnowledgeV2 L4 fallthrough' at priority=1 (low_confidence) AND 'General L4 fallthrough with no emotional context' at priority=0 (out_of_scope) — but does not define what distinguishes the two categories or state which of SAD §4.1 (reason='out_of_scope') and SAD §4.2 Trigger B (reason='low_confidence') is normative for the same L4 code path. This ambiguity is a carry-forward of the pre-existing SAD inconsistency, not a new regression, and is non-blocking at this phase.
citations: ADR.md Index — all 13 ADR-P2-01..ADR-P2-13 listed as Accepted
ADR-P2-05 Decision — 7-step L3 order matches SAD §3.9 SAD Conformance Constraint and §4.1 L3 branch
ADR-P2-06 Decision — Option A keywords + 0.2×count formula matches SAD §3.7 EmotionClassifier sub-module
ADR-P2-10 Decision table — priority mapping matches SAD §3.11 Architectural Decision block
ADR-P2-12 Decision — two new tables + ivfflat + column activations match SAD §5.1–§5.2 and SRS FR-23
ADR-P2-02 Rationale — hnsw explicitly deferred with O(n) vs O(n log n) build-speed argument
ADR-P2-06 Context — Options B/C documented with latency/dep table before selecting A
ADR-P2-10 Trigger column — 'HybridKnowledgeV2 L4 fallthrough' at priority=1 vs 'General L4 fallthrough' at priority=0 (ambiguous distinction)

gaps.description: ADR-P2-10 Decision table assigns 'HybridKnowledgeV2 L4 fallthrough' to priority=1 (reason='low_confidence', 15 min SLA) but also defines a separate priority=0 trigger as 'General L4 fallthrough with no emotional context' (reason='out_of_scope', 30 min SLA). The ADR does not define what distinguishes a 'general' L4 fallthrough from the HybridKnowledgeV2 L4 fallthrough, nor does it declare which of SAD §4.1 (L4 → reason='out_of_scope') and SAD §4.2 Trigger B (L4 → reason='low_confidence') is normative. The pre-existing SAD §4.1 vs §4.2 inconsistency is carried forward without explicit resolution.
